events.text: Margaret Martin, 73-year-old Other female, Current smoker (0.5 ppd). Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.
Diagnosed with High-grade serous carcinoma of the Right ovary. Clinical staging: T3cN0M0, AJCC Stage IIIC. ICD-10: C56.9. Stage IIIC high-grade serous ovarian cancer with BRCA1 mutation.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for baseline staging.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.

Underwent primary debulking surgery: Total abdominal hysterectomy, bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy, omentectomy, and optimal cytoreduction (residual disease < 1 cm). Estimated blood loss 250 mL. Length of stay 5 days. Uncomplicated postoperative course.
Pathology specimen received for surgical pathology.
Clinical visit for postoperative follow-up.

Initiated first-line chemotherapy with anti-angiogenic: Carboplatin AUC 6 + Paclitaxel 175 mg/m2 q3w x 6 cycles + Bevacizumab 15 mg/kg q3w. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.

Initiated first-line chemotherapy with anti-angiogenic: Carboplatin AUC 6 + Paclitaxel 175 mg/m2 q3w x 6 cycles + Bevacizumab 15 mg/kg q3w. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.

Initiated first-line chemotherapy with anti-angiogenic: Carboplatin AUC 6 + Paclitaxel 175 mg/m2 q3w x 6 cycles + Bevacizumab 15 mg/kg q3w. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 3 neutropenia. Treated with G-CSF (filgrastim 5 mcg/kg daily x 5 days). CBC monitored closely.

Initiated first-line chemotherapy with anti-angiogenic: Carboplatin AUC 6 + Paclitaxel 175 mg/m2 q3w x 6 cycles + Bevacizumab 15 mg/kg q3w. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
Developed grade 2 fatigue. Activity modification recommended. TSH and CBC checked - within normal limits.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for restaging after first-line chemotherapy with anti-angiogenic.
Clinical visit for results review after first-line chemotherapy with anti-angiogenic.
Initiated alternative PARP inhibitor maintenance: Niraparib 200 mg daily maintenance. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for follow-up on alternative PARP inhibitor maintenance.
Clinical visit for follow-up on alternative PARP inhibitor maintenance.
PET/CT performed for restaging on alternative PARP inhibitor maintenance.
PET/CT performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
PET/CT performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.

documents.text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Margaret Martin
MRN: patient_150ab7a3f646
Date of Study: 2023-05-18

CLINICAL INDICATION: High-grade serous carcinoma of the right ovary. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Clear. No pulmonary nodules or masses. No pleural effusion.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: Complex cystic and solid mass in the right adnexa measuring 9.9 cm. Omental caking present. Moderate ascites. Peritoneal carcinomatosis.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Complex adnexal mass with peritoneal carcinomatosis and ascites, concerning for advanced ovarian malignancy.

Electronically signed by: Robert Chang, MD
Department of Radiology

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Margaret Martin
MRN: patient_150ab7a3f646
Specimen ID: S2023-29687
Date of Procedure: 2023-05-20
Date of Report: 2023-05-23

SPECIMEN: Right ovary, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
High-grade serous carcinoma, poorly differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 5.6 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.5 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Present

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
PAX8: Positive
WT1: Positive
P53: Aberrant (overexpression)
CK7: Positive
CK20: Negative

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Margaret Martin - Right ovary" is a tan-pink tissue fragment measuring 5.6 x 3.9 x 2.8 cm. The cut surface reveals a firm gray-white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A4.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a poorly differentiated neoplasm composed of cords and trabeculae of atypical cells with moderate cytoplasm and high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio. Mitotic figures are identified (6 per 10 HPF). No necrosis is identified. The surrounding stroma shows desmoplastic reaction.

Electronically signed by: Michael Torres, MD
Department of Pathology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Medical Oncology

Patient: Margaret Martin
MRN: patient_150ab7a3f646
Date: 2023-05-28
Provider: Dr. Helen Park

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Martin is a 73-year-old female who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed high-grade serous carcinoma of the right ovary, IIIC. Patient was referred by primary care physician after abnormal imaging. Stage IIIC high-grade serous ovarian cancer with BRCA1 mutation. Pathology confirmed high-grade serous carcinoma. Molecular testing has been sent. Patient reports fatigue and unintentional 10-lb weight loss over 3 months.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- gastroesophageal reflux disease
- hypertension

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- Appendectomy (age 25)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Current smoker (0.5 ppd)
- Alcohol: Former heavy drinker, quit 5 years ago
- Occupation: Office manager
- Lives with spouse

FAMILY HISTORY:
- Mother with breast cancer at age 62.

ALLERGIES: NKDA

MEDICATIONS:
- Omeprazole 20 mg daily
- Lisinopril 10 mg daily

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: mild peripheral edema, abdominal bloating, joint pain, intermittent headaches. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 126/76, HR 73, RR 19, Temp 97.3F, SpO2 97% on RA, Wt 68.7 kg
General: Alert, oriented, well-appearing, in no acute distress.
HEENT: NCAT, EOMI, PERRL. Oral mucosa moist without lesions.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Diminished breath sounds at right base. No wheezes.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatosplenomegaly.
Extremities: No edema. No cyanosis or clubbing.
Neuro: Alert and oriented. No focal neurological deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 9.5, Hgb 10.6, Plt 259, Cr 0.74

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
73-year-old female with IIIC high-grade serous carcinoma of the right ovary. initial oncology consultation.

1. High-grade serous carcinoma of the right ovary, Stage IIIC
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend primary debulking surgery
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - Peripheral access adequate
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Amanda Foster, MD
Medical Oncology

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Margaret Martin
MRN: patient_150ab7a3f646
Date of Report: 2023-06-09
Specimen: Right ovary (Core needle biopsy)
Diagnosis: High-grade serous carcinoma
Panel: Guardant360 CDx (648 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 3.6 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  BRCA1 p.E1836fs (c.5503delA) - Frameshift (Exon 22) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  TP53 p.C176Y - Missense (Exon 5)
  NF1 p.R1947* - Nonsense (Exon 39)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  CDK12 p.T826A - VUS
  ARID1A p.D1850N - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - BRCA1 pathogenic variant: FDA-approved PARP inhibitors include olaparib, niraparib, rucaparib.
  - Platinum sensitivity expected.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 669x coverage.
Tumor content: 47%.

Electronically signed by: Andrew Patel, MD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Margaret Martin
MRN: patient_150ab7a3f646
Specimen ID: S2023-57101
Date of Procedure: 2023-06-20
Date of Report: 2023-06-22

SPECIMEN: Right ovary, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
High-grade serous carcinoma, well differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 6.3 cm
Margins: Positive at inked margin
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
PAX8: Positive
WT1: Positive
P53: Aberrant (overexpression)
CK7: Positive
CK20: Negative

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Margaret Martin - Right ovary" is a gray-white tissue fragment measuring 6.3 x 4.4 x 3.1 cm. The cut surface reveals a soft yellow-tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A10.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a well differentiated neoplasm composed of cords and trabeculae of atypical cells with abundant cytoplasm and prominent nucleoli. Mitotic figures are frequent (12 per 10 HPF). No necrosis is identified. The surrounding stroma shows minimal reactive changes.

Electronically signed by: James Park, MD
Department of Pathology

PET/CT REPORT

Patient: Margaret Martin
MRN: patient_150ab7a3f646
Date of Study: 2024-10-29

CLINICAL INDICATION: High-grade serous carcinoma of the right ovary. surveillance imaging.

COMPARISON: Prior study dated approximately 8 weeks ago available for comparison.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Clear. No pulmonary nodules or masses. No pleural effusion.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: Complex cystic and solid mass in the right adnexa measuring 9.4 cm. Omental caking present. Moderate ascites. Peritoneal carcinomatosis.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Complex adnexal mass with peritoneal carcinomatosis and ascites, concerning for advanced ovarian malignancy.
2. Overall assessment: Partial response per RECIST 1.1.

Electronically signed by: David Kim, MD
Department of Radiology
